contract.rationale: FHIR Consent is allowed only as a representation of the governed pharmacy consent checkpoint, never the owning decision itself.

identifierPolicy.label: Consent scope fingerprint
identifierPolicy.description: FHIR Consent identifiers bind the chosen provider, scope hash, and consent checkpoint generation.
identifierPolicy.versionRule: Withdrawal or supersession issues a new version tied to the next checkpoint state.

cardinalityPolicy.label: Single consent per checkpoint version
cardinalityPolicy.description: Each PharmacyConsentCheckpoint version emits one current Consent row with append-only supersession.

redactionPolicy.label: Minimal partner interchange
redactionPolicy.description: Only partner-required structured fields and declared profile elements may cross the boundary.

companionArtifactPolicy.label: Partner exchange manifest
companionArtifactPolicy.description: External bundles may carry an omission/redaction manifest that traces every excluded artifact or field to policy.

replayPolicy.label: Stable referral by package hash
replayPolicy.description: The same pharmacy package hash, consent checkpoint, and provider tuple must rematerialize the same referral set.

supersessionPolicy.label: Consent invalidation on checkpoint drift
supersessionPolicy.description: Consent drift, withdrawal, or renewed checkpoint invalidates the prior current Consent row and requires a new representation set.

callbackCorrelationPolicy.label: No callback correlation
callbackCorrelationPolicy.description: Clinical persistence-only contracts do not participate in partner callback correlation.
